pr.title: Match CObj setupTopHalfCamera, bring in existing matches for CObjEraseScreen and CObjLoad
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched more of sysdolphin baselib camera-object code by replacing CObj assembly/NOT_IMPLEMENTED areas with C, especially setupTopHalfCamera, HSD_CObjEraseScreen, and CObjLoad. The PR also reshaped CObj descriptor/header types into common/frustum/perspective camera-desc structs, renamed viewport fields from left/right/top/bottom to xmin/xmax/ymin/ymax, updated dependent users in lbvector and grIzumi, and made setup camera helpers static C-file internals.
what_changed: Replaced the previous MUST_MATCH inline assembly block for HSD_CObjEraseScreen with C code that early-returns on null CObj or no enabled erase channels, computes mid-depth from near/far, handles perspective/frustum/ortho projection cases, and calls HSD_EraseRect.
Implemented setupTopHalfCamera as a static bool helper using HSD_VIGetRenderMode, viewport clipping against efbHeight, GXSetScissor, GXSetViewport, C_MTXFrustum/C_MTXOrtho, and GXSetProjection.
Implemented setupBottomHalfCamera similarly for bottom render-pass behavior, including screen_top = efbHeight - 8 clipping and adjusted projection math.
Converted setupNormalCamera and setupBottomHalfCamera prototypes from exported int helpers to static bool helpers local to cobj.c; corresponding declarations were removed from cobj.h.

Implemented CObjLoad from a descriptor: reset flags, set viewport/scissor, initialize eye/interest WObjs, set near/far, choose up-vector vs roll, and dispatch projection setup for perspective, ortho, or frustum.

Refactored CObj descriptor layout in cobj.h: HSD_CObjDesc became a union over HSD_CameraDescCommon, HSD_CameraDescFrustum, and HSD_CameraDescPerspective; forward.h was updated to forward-declare the union and new structs.
Introduced reusable HSD_RectS16, HSD_RectF32, and Scissor typedefs, and changed HSD_CObj.viewport to HSD_RectF32 and scissor to Scissor.
Renamed viewport field users from left/right/top/bottom to xmin/xmax/ymin/ymax in CObj setup code and lbVector_WorldToScreen.
Changed grIzumi ReflectCObjDesc and lb_80013B14 prototype to use HSD_CameraDescPerspective rather than the generic HSD_CObjDesc layout.
Cleaned up several cobj.c matching aids by using literal constants such as 0.0f, 1.0f, -1.0f, FLT_MIN, and direct HSD_ASSERT/HSD_Panic calls instead of local string/float extern aliases.
smart_moves: Made the descriptor model match observed data more explicitly: perspective descriptors no longer fake a generic union initializer and instead use a HSD_CameraDescPerspective type with fov/aspect fields.
Centralized rectangle semantics by using xmin/xmax/ymin/ymax for viewport rectangles, reducing ambiguity compared with left/right/top/bottom when the same code computes widths and heights.
Kept matching-sensitive helpers static in cobj.c instead of exposing internal setupNormalCamera/setupTopHalfCamera/setupBottomHalfCamera prototypes through the header.
Preserved matching artifacts where useful, such as unused stack arrays and unusual assignments in setupTopHalfCamera, rather than over-cleaning the C into nonmatching idiomatic code.
Removed large inline assembly for HSD_CObjEraseScreen once the C formulation matched, improving maintainability while retaining projection-specific behavior.
Used CObjLoad to drive descriptor interpretation through typed common/perspective/frustum/ortho views, making future descriptor matches less dependent on raw offsets.
decomp_lessons: For baselib camera code, descriptor structs may need to be split by projection flavor instead of represented as one broad struct with an imprecise union; callers like grIzumi can reveal which concrete descriptor form is correct.
Viewport naming matters for matching and readability: xmin/xmax/ymin/ymax makes width/height calculations and screen clipping code clearer than generic left/right/top/bottom.
Internal setup helpers that are not real exported APIs should stay static in the .c file; exporting them in headers can misrepresent linkage and invite incorrect dependencies.
Replacing assembly with C may require matching-oriented control flow, return style, and temporary variables, not just equivalent high-level behavior.

When CObj code references projection fields by union offset, verify the projection case before assigning semantic field names; the top/bottom camera implementation still uses perspective-named fields in frustum/ortho formulas because that is what the observed layout/diff supported.
Header cleanup can be part of matching work: typedefs, forward declarations, method macros, and exact function signatures all affect downstream code and decomp accuracy.
naming_conventions: Use HSD_RectS16 for signed 16-bit rectangle descriptor values with xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax.
Use HSD_RectF32 for float viewport rectangles with xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax.
Use Scissor for u16 scissor rectangles with left, right, top, bottom.
Use HSD_CameraDescCommon, HSD_CameraDescFrustum, and HSD_CameraDescPerspective for projection-specific camera descriptor layouts.
HSD_CObjDesc is a union, not a struct, when it needs to provide common, frustum, ortho, and perspective views.
Use nnear and ffar field names in camera descriptor structs to avoid colliding with near/far identifiers while preserving meaning.
Name CObj casting/method macros as HSD_COBJ, HSD_COBJ_INFO, and HSD_COBJ_METHOD.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed a MUST_MATCH asm implementation of HSD_CObjEraseScreen after finding C that matched the projection switch and HSD_EraseRect argument order.
Used early return structure in HSD_CObjEraseScreen for null/no-enabled-channel cases, matching the assembly branch pattern more directly than nesting the whole function body.
Used literal constants directly where they produced matching code, replacing extern float aliases such as HSD_CObj_804DE478/HSD_CObj_804DE47C in viewport setup.
Kept unused local arrays in camera setup functions, apparently as stack-layout/codegen aids.
Converted helper return types from int to bool while still returning 0/true where the original branch shape required it.
Used static inline helper CObjResetFlags and HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtrDirect to express small operations in a way compatible with matching and header organization.

review_feedback.feedback: Move static functions from the .h to the top of the .c; put the static keyword on the declaration, and either keep or omit it on the definition.
No line-level review comments or PR body were present in the slice, so intent beyond the title, diff, and single review note is limited.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching HSD_CObjSetUpVector or HSD_CObjGetUpVector, which remained NOT_IMPLEMENTED in this slice.
Search for remaining users of old viewport.left/right/top/bottom field names after the HSD_RectF32 rename.
Search for other HSD_CObjDesc initializers that should be converted to HSD_CameraDescPerspective or HSD_CameraDescFrustum.
Check subsequent cobj.c PRs for cleanup or validation of setupBottomHalfCamera projection field names and matching artifacts.
Look for remaining MUST_MATCH assembly blocks in sysdolphin/baselib that can be replaced with C using the same constant and control-flow tactics.
